core_mechanism: A Hopf-algebra (quantum-group) symmetry acting through a braided, non-cocommutative coproduct on a braided/noncommutative product, whose compatibility conditions force covariance constraints — linear (Ward-identity-type) relations — among the theory's correlation/averaged quantities
brief_reason: The abstract's method rests on imposing Hopf-algebra symmetries on a braided product and deriving, via four algebraic compatibility conditions, non-perturbative linear relations among correlation functions.
